name: Concussion (Variant 19)
description: A traumatic brain injury induced by biomechanical forces.
mechanism: Rapid acceleration/deceleration of the brain within the skull.
diagnostic_tips: Neurocognitive testing. CT/MRI usually normal but used to rule out bleeds.

pt_plan.acute: Physical and cognitive rest (24-48 hrs).
pt_plan.subacute: Symptom-limited aerobic exercise (Buffalo Concussion Treadmill Test), Vestibular rehab.
pt_plan.return_to_sport: Graduated return to play protocol (6 steps).

name: Turf Toe (Variant 19)
description: Sprain of the plantar capsule and first metatarsophalangeal (MTP) joint.
mechanism: Forceful hyperextension of the big toe.
signs_symptoms: Pain at the base of the big toe
msk_tests: First MTP mobility assessment (looking for laxity and pain provocation)
diagnostic_tips: X-ray to rule out fracture or sesamoid involvement. MRI for severity of capsular tear.

pt_plan.acute: RICE, taping/stiff insoles to limit extension, crutches if severe.
pt_plan.subacute: Gentle ROM, intrinsic foot strengthening.
pt_plan.return_to_sport: Proprioceptive training, progressive running. Taping for return to play.

name: Patellar Tendinopathy (Jumper's Knee) (Variant 19)
description: Pain in the patellar tendon due to repetitive overloading, common in sports requiring jumping.
mechanism: Repetitive eccentric loading of the knee extensor mechanism.
signs_symptoms: Localized pain at the inferior pole of the patella
diagnostic_tips: Pain remains localized to the tendon. US or MRI can confirm tendinosis.

pt_plan.acute: Isometrics (heavy slow resistance for pain relief).
pt_plan.subacute: Isotonics progressing to eccentrics.
pt_plan.return_to_sport: Energy storage exercises (plyometrics), sport-specific jumps.

name: Lateral Epicondylalgia (Tennis Elbow) (Variant 19)
description: Overuse condition involving the extensor tendons of the forearm, primarily the extensor carpi radialis brevis.
mechanism: Microtrauma to the extensor tendon origin at the lateral epicondyle.
diagnostic_tips: Clinical diagnosis usually sufficient. Ultrasound can show tendon thickening or tearing.

pt_plan.acute: Relative rest, counterforce bracing, ice.
pt_plan.subacute: Eccentric wrist extension exercises, soft tissue mobilization.
pt_plan.return_to_sport: Gradual return to hitting, equipment modification (string tension, grip size).

signs_symptoms: Pain at the base of the big toe
msk_tests: First MTP mobility assessment (looking for laxity and pain provocation)
diagnostic_tips: X-ray to rule out fracture or sesamoid involvement. MRI for severity of capsular tear.

pt_plan.acute: RICE, taping/stiff insoles to limit extension, crutches if severe.
pt_plan.subacute: Gentle ROM, intrinsic foot strengthening.
pt_plan.return_to_sport: Proprioceptive training, progressive running. Taping for return to play.

name: Patellar Tendinopathy (Jumper's Knee) (Variant 7)
description: Pain in the patellar tendon due to repetitive overloading, common in sports requiring jumping.
mechanism: Repetitive eccentric loading of the knee extensor mechanism.
signs_symptoms: Localized pain at the inferior pole of the patella
diagnostic_tips: Pain remains localized to the tendon. US or MRI can confirm tendinosis.

pt_plan.acute: Isometrics (heavy slow resistance for pain relief).
pt_plan.subacute: Isotonics progressing to eccentrics.
pt_plan.return_to_sport: Energy storage exercises (plyometrics), sport-specific jumps.

name: Lateral Epicondylalgia (Tennis Elbow) (Variant 7)
description: Overuse condition involving the extensor tendons of the forearm, primarily the extensor carpi radialis brevis.
mechanism: Microtrauma to the extensor tendon origin at the lateral epicondyle.
diagnostic_tips: Clinical diagnosis usually sufficient. Ultrasound can show tendon thickening or tearing.

pt_plan.acute: Relative rest, counterforce bracing, ice.
pt_plan.subacute: Eccentric wrist extension exercises, soft tissue mobilization.
pt_plan.return_to_sport: Gradual return to hitting, equipment modification (string tension, grip size).

name: Concussion (Variant 6)
description: A traumatic brain injury induced by biomechanical forces.
mechanism: Rapid acceleration/deceleration of the brain within the skull.
diagnostic_tips: Neurocognitive testing. CT/MRI usually normal but used to rule out bleeds.

pt_plan.acute: Physical and cognitive rest (24-48 hrs).
pt_plan.subacute: Symptom-limited aerobic exercise (Buffalo Concussion Treadmill Test), Vestibular rehab.
pt_plan.return_to_sport: Graduated return to play protocol (6 steps).

name: Hamstring Strain
description: Strain or tear of the hamstring muscles, most commonly the biceps femoris during high-speed running.
causes: Eccentric overload during terminal swing phase
mechanism: High-speed running or overstretching (e.g., high kick).
signs_symptoms: Sudden sharp pain in posterior thigh
msk_tests: Pain with passive straight leg raise
diagnostic_tips: Ultrasound for acute evaluation; MRI for grading and prognosis. Localize max tenderness.

pt_plan.acute: Ice, compression, frequent pain-free submaximal isometrics.
pt_plan.subacute: Eccentric strengthening (Nordic curls), sliding exercises.
pt_plan.return_to_sport: High-speed sprinting progression, sport-specific agility, LSI >90%.

name: Lateral Ankle Sprain
description: Injury to lateral ligament complex (ATFL, CFL, PTFL), with ATFL most commonly affected.
mechanism: Inversion and plantarflexion.
diagnostic_tips: Use Ottawa Ankle Rules to rule out fracture before X-ray.

pt_plan.acute: PRICE, early weight-bearing with brace, ankle pumps.
pt_plan.subacute: Proprioception training (wobble board), peroneal muscle strengthening.
pt_plan.return_to_sport: Sport-specific agility, bracing/taping during play, lateral hop tests.

name: Tennis Elbow (Lateral Epicondylalgia)
description: Overuse tendinopathy of the common extensor origin, primarily the ECRB.
mechanism: Eccentric overload of wrist extensors.
diagnostic_tips: Pain localized 1-2 cm distal to lateral epicondyle.

pt_plan.acute: Activity modification, counterforce bracing, isometric holds.
pt_plan.subacute: Heavy slow resistance (HSR) or eccentric training.
pt_plan.return_to_sport: Technique correction (stroke mechanics), equipment modification.

name: SLAP Lesion
description: Superior Labrum Anterior and Posterior tear, often involving the biceps anchor.
mechanism: Peel-back mechanism during late cocking phase of throwing.
diagnostic_tips: MRA is the most accurate imaging modality. Often coexists with biceps tendinopathy.

pt_plan.acute: Decrease overhead activities, NSAIDs, early periscapular stabilization.
pt_plan.subacute: Rotator cuff strengthening (especially IRs to balance ER), core integration.
pt_plan.return_to_sport: Interval throwing program, biomechanical analysis.

pt_plan.subacute: Heavy slow resistance (HSR) or eccentric training.
pt_plan.return_to_sport: Technique correction (stroke mechanics), equipment modification.

name: SLAP Lesion (Variant 104)
description: Superior Labrum Anterior and Posterior tear, often involving the biceps anchor.
mechanism: Peel-back mechanism during late cocking phase of throwing.
diagnostic_tips: MRA is the most accurate imaging modality. Often coexists with biceps tendinopathy.

pt_plan.acute: Decrease overhead activities, NSAIDs, early periscapular stabilization.
pt_plan.subacute: Rotator cuff strengthening (especially IRs to balance ER), core integration.
pt_plan.return_to_sport: Interval throwing program, biomechanical analysis.
